description: Receipt verification MUST fail when receipt.registry_did does not bind to the authority the context was fetched from (RFC-ACDP-0010 §8 step 2). A receipt is only evidence about the registry that served it; accepting a receipt naming a different authority would let a hostile registry replay another registry's receipts (or mint receipts impersonating one). Same authority-binding principle as fed-006.

input.scenario: Consumer fetches GET https://hostile.example/contexts/acdp%3A%2F%2Fhostile.example%2F… and the envelope carries a receipt whose registry_did names registry.example.com

expected.behavior: §8 step 2: registry_did MUST be did:web:<authority> where <authority> equals the authority the response was fetched from. did:web:registry.example.com ≠ hostile.example → the receipt MUST be reported as failed regardless of whether its signature verifies under registry.example.com's key. The same check rejects the inverse case (receipt.registry_did = did:web:hostile.example but ctx_id/origin_registry authority = registry.example.com — the intra-receipt consistency rule of RFC-ACDP-0010 §4).

additional_failure_cases.case: registry_did uses a method other than did:web (e.g. did:key)
additional_failure_cases.expected_outcome: failure — registry_did is did:web-only in 0.2.0 (RFC-ACDP-0010 §4); registries are DNS-bound servers.
failure — the receipt key must belong to the minting registry's DID document (§8 step 1/2).
